title: Atlanta Beltline Fest at Pittsburgh Yards — Saturday
description: Free two-day festival at Pittsburgh Yards (James Bridges Field). Live music and DJs, local + international food vendors, art along the Beltline, and the World Cup on the big screen. Easy, walkable, low-pressure way to spend a whole Saturday together — grab a bite, catch a set, dance a little, watch a match. 10 AM–7 PM both days; Saturday is the bigger day.
budget: FREE entry; food and drinks à la carte
location: Pittsburgh Yards — James Bridges Field, 352 University Ave SW, Atlanta, GA
vibe: Festive, sunny, social — for couples who like energy without pretense

title: The Garden Party ATL — Variety Show at Bambellas Art World
description: A variety show with live music, comedy, and burlesque at Bambellas Art World, a creative studio/gallery space. Doors 7 PM, show 7–11 PM. Hits three shared interests in one night: paintings/art on the walls, live music on stage, and comedy between sets. Intimate, expressive, very Atlanta — and tonight only (Saturday).
budget: Ticketed at the door (check HappeningNext for current price)
location: BAMBELLAS ART WORLD, Atlanta, GA (details on event page)
vibe: Bohemian, sensual, fun — for couples who want culture, laughs, and a little edge

title: Vegan Soul Sunday (Father's Day Edition) + Gallery Hop
description: Sunday plan: Vegan Soul Sunday at New Life Presbyterian Church, 12–4 PM — free, plant-based soul food, holistic vendors, Father’s Day edition. Pair it with a stop at a standing gallery show afterward (Seeds of Resistance opens Mon 6/22 at SFQP, or current shows at The Bakery / VINSONart). Vegetarian-friendly and art-forward without feeling try-hard.
budget: FREE entry to Vegan Soul Sunday; gallery visits free or low-cost
location: New Life Presbyterian Church, Atlanta (start); gallery TBA
vibe: Warm, rooted, easygoing — for couples who like good food and slow looking
